what_it_does: Implements the Stepper as a function component using React.createElement (h). Renders a div with class cv-stepper-line, mapping each step to a div.cv-step whose state class is is-done (index < active), is-active (index === active), or empty (upcoming). Each step contains a span.cv-step__dot showing a checkmark for done steps or the 1-based number otherwise, and a span.cv-step__label with the step's label. Steps may be strings or {label} objects.
observations: state = i < active ? 'is-done' : i === active ? 'is-active' : '' — three-way classification by index
dot content: i < active ? '✓' : String(i + 1) (1-based numbering)
label derived from object.label or the raw string
className merges caller class via filter(Boolean).join(' '); spreads ...rest onto the wrapper
Uses global React with const h = React.createElement (no JSX)
standouts: Visual states (done/active/upcoming, gold dots) are entirely expressed through token CSS classes; the JS only computes which class and whether the dot is a check or a number
conventions: Token-class wrapper carrying no inline styles or literals (one-home design discipline)
concerns: key={i} (array index) rather than a stable id — acceptable for a static step list but would mis-key if steps reorder
notes: A token-class-only linear stepper; index versus active drives done/active/upcoming classes and check-vs-number dots.
purpose_vs_actual: Match — implements the done/active/upcoming model the header comment and .d.ts describe.
intention_signals: To render a numbered linear progress trail as a structural shell over design-system CSS, with the gold-dot/done semantics living in tokens and the JS only deciding per-step state.
novelty: Buildless React.createElement authoring with the checkmark/number dot logic inline.
contribution: Contributes the structural implementation of the linear Stepper, including the three-way index-based state classification and the check-vs-number dot rule, delegated to .cv-stepper-line/.cv-step token classes.
summary_for_embedding: React.createElement implementation of a linear Stepper rendering token-class steps with done/active/upcoming states by index and check-or-number dots.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the buildless, token-class-only numbered linear stepper whose visual states live in design-system CSS.
